pattern: Since every operation can nudge one untouched element anywhere within k of its own value, picking a target value and asking 'how many elements already equal it, plus how many others sit within k of it' turns the operation-budget puzzle into a sliding window over the sorted array.
hints: Sort the array. For any candidate target value, every element already equal to it is free, and every OTHER element within distance k of it could be converted to it by spending one operation on it.
For a candidate target value v, count how many elements fall in the closed range [v - k, v + k]; subtract however many are already exactly v to get how many would need an operation to convert.

The best achievable frequency for target v is (elements already equal to v) plus the smaller of (numOperations) and (elements in range but not already equal). Try every value actually present in the array as the candidate target and keep the best result.

edge_inputs.description: zero operations allowed, answer is just the existing max frequency
zero range, an operation can only add zero so it never helps
numOperations equal to the array length
the best target is not the most frequent existing value but a nearby one reachable by more elements
two separated clusters where only one is worth converting into

stress.description: random arrays with random k and numOperations within bounds
stress.generator_python:
def gen(rng, size):
    n = rng.randint(1, max(1, min(size, 20000)))
    nums = [rng.randint(1, 100000) for _ in range(n)]
    k = rng.randint(0, 100000)
    numOperations = rng.randint(0, n)
    return [nums, k, numOperations]

solutions.python:
import bisect
class Solution:
    def maxFrequency(self, nums, k, numOperations):
        nums = sorted(nums)
        n = len(nums)
        best = 0
        for i in range(n):
            v = nums[i]
            lo = bisect.bisect_left(nums, v - k)
            hi = bisect.bisect_right(nums, v + k) - 1
            count = hi - lo + 1
            eq_lo = bisect.bisect_left(nums, v)
            eq_hi = bisect.bisect_right(nums, v)
            cnt_equal = eq_hi - eq_lo
            freq = cnt_equal + min(numOperations, count - cnt_equal)
            if freq > best:
                best = freq
        return best
